brant-point.name: Brant Point
brant-point.description: The harbor's front porch. Walking distance to town with iconic lighthouse views and some of the island's most coveted waterfront.
highlights: Walking distance to Main Street and ferries
R-1 zoning — smaller lots, higher density

shoreline.concerns: Harbor-side erosion minimal. Jetties Beach renourishment ongoing. Flood zone considerations for waterfront parcels.

brant-point.whatsHappening: Several high-end renovations in progress. Two new construction applications pending with HDC.

sconset.name: 'Sconset
sconset.description: The village at the end of the island. Rose-covered cottages, dramatic bluff views, and a fiercely independent character that makes it Nantucket's most coveted address.

shoreline.concerns: Bluff erosion remains the defining issue. Geotube installations ongoing. Several properties have lost significant setback in recent years. Flood insurance costs rising.

sconset.whatsHappening: Multiple renovation applications in process. Commission watching bluff setback compliance closely. Inventory near historic lows.

madaket.name: Madaket
madaket.description: The sunset side. Remote, windswept, and deeply local. Madaket rewards those who want distance from town and proximity to some of the island's most dramatic coastline.
highlights: Famous sunset views over Madaket Harbor
Remote western location — 6 miles from town
Larger lots, more affordable entry points

shoreline.concerns: Smith's Point erosion continues. Beach migration shifting sand patterns. Dredging projects in Hither Creek area. Some parcels facing long-term viability questions.

madaket.whatsHappening: Erosion conversations are cooling demand slightly in exposed areas, but protected harbor-side lots remain strong.

mid-island.name: Mid-Island
mid-island.description: The island's practical center. Schools, hospital, and daily services are here. Larger lots and more permissive zoning make it the focus of workforce housing and development discussions.
highlights: Largest lots and most permissive zoning (LUG-2)
Best buyer leverage on the island currently
mid-island.whatsHappening: Planning Board reviewing workforce housing subdivision proposals. Zoning density debates ongoing. Ground cover calculation changes under review.

town.name: Town
town.description: The historic heart. Cobblestone streets, whaling-era architecture, and the strictest HDC oversight on the island. Every renovation is a conversation with history.
highlights: Very high HDC scrutiny — 4-8 month permit timeline
town.whatsHappening: Steady renovation activity. Historic homes trading at full ask. Limited turnover means low inventory despite strong demand.

cliff.name: Cliff
cliff.description: Trophy territory. The Cliff neighborhood commands the island's highest per-transaction prices with sweeping Sound views, established estates, and a quiet remove from town.
highlights: Highest average sale prices on the island
Pre-market activity intense — many trades happen off-MLS

shoreline.concerns: Bluff stability varies by parcel. North-facing properties generally stable. Steps Beach access subject to seasonal conditions.

cliff.whatsHappening: Trophy properties moving in days, not weeks. Pre-market activity is intense in the $10M+ segment.

surfside.name: Surfside
surfside.description: South shore energy. Popular beach, accessible location, and a mix of established homes and new construction. Surfside balances beach lifestyle with relative proximity to town.
highlights: Popular Surfside Beach — walkable for many properties
Mix of established homes and new construction

shoreline.concerns: South shore exposure means ongoing beach dynamics. Surfside Beach stable but sand movement varies year to year. Flood zone mapping affects insurance costs for beachfront parcels.

surfside.whatsHappening: Seasonal buyers arriving early this year. Beach access lots are the prize. Several new construction approvals in pipeline.

cisco.name: Cisco
cisco.description: The south shore's quieter neighbor. Cisco and the Hummock Pond area offer larger lots, brewery culture, and a laid-back energy that attracts a different kind of buyer.
highlights: Hummock Pond access and nature trails

shoreline.concerns: Hummock Pond breach dynamics affect nearby properties periodically. South shore exposure similar to Surfside but less developed.

cisco.whatsHappening: Waiting for summer momentum. Good value window for buyers right now.

dionis.name: Dionis
dionis.description: North shore serenity. Calm beaches, spectacular water views, and a family-friendly atmosphere. Dionis waterfront is generating serious interest from development-minded buyers.
highlights: Calm north shore beaches — ideal for families
R-5 zoning — larger lots with moderate oversight

shoreline.concerns: North shore generally more protected than south. Dionis Beach access well-maintained. Minimal erosion concerns for most parcels.

dionis.whatsHappening: Waterfront parcels generating serious interest from development-minded buyers. Several lot inquiries in the last quarter.

polpis.name: Polpis
polpis.description: The quiet corridor. Polpis Road connects Town to the eastern end of the island, winding through conservation land, harbor views, and some of Nantucket's most private estates.
highlights: R-5 zoning — large lots, moderate HDC
polpis.whatsHappening: Limited turnover. Properties trade privately. Conservation easements shape development potential significantly.

monomoy.name: Monomoy
monomoy.description: The quiet prestige. Tucked between Town and Brant Point, Monomoy offers waterfront living with harbor views and easy access to town — without the foot traffic.

shoreline.concerns: Harbor-side location provides good protection. Minimal erosion concerns. Flood zone considerations for lowest-lying parcels.

monomoy.whatsHappening: Pool house and accessory structure applications trending. Ground cover limits being tested on several parcels.

madequecham.name: Madequecham
madequecham.description: The south shore's hidden stretch. Between Surfside and Cisco, Madequecham offers dramatic bluff-top properties with some of the island's most powerful ocean views.
highlights: Less developed than Surfside or Cisco

shoreline.concerns: Bluff erosion is the primary concern. Several properties have lost setback distance over the past decade. Geotube and dune restoration projects ongoing. Long-term viability assessment recommended for bluff-edge parcels.

madequecham.whatsHappening: Erosion mitigation projects in progress. Buyers with long-term vision see opportunity; cautious buyers are steering inland.
